faqs.question: यह कविता है या कहानी? What is the difference?
faqs.answer: This chapter is a कविता (kavita) - a poem, not a कहानी (kahani) - a story. A poem uses rhythm (लय), rhyming (तुकबंदी), and special arrangement of words in lines (पंक्तियाँ). A story is written in paragraphs and narrates events. Poems are usually shorter and more musical. This poem 'नाव बनाओ नाव बनाओ' has a song-like quality with its repeating lines and matching sounds.

faqs.question: कागज़ की नाव कैसे बनाते हैं?
faqs.answer: Making a paper boat is easy and fun! Take a rectangular piece of paper. Fold it in half from top to bottom. Then fold the top corners down to meet at the centre, making a triangle shape. Fold the bottom edges up on both sides. Open the bottom and flatten it into a square. Fold the bottom corners up to make a triangle again. Finally, gently pull the top corners apart and the boat shape appears! Float it in water and watch it sail!

faqs.question: 'नाव बनाओ' में 'बनाओ' किस प्रकार की क्रिया है?
faqs.answer: बनाओ is an आज्ञा वाचक क्रिया (imperative verb) - a command or instruction. It tells someone to do something. 'बनाओ' means 'Make!' The basic verb is बनाना (to make). When you change it to बनाओ, it becomes a friendly command. Other examples from the poem: तैराओ (Float it!), चलाओ (Sail it!). These imperative forms are used when talking to friends or people of similar age using तुम.

faqs.question: कविता में कल्पना (imagination) क्या भूमिका निभाती है?
faqs.answer: Imagination (कल्पना) is the heart of this poem! The paper boat is just a small folded piece of paper, but through imagination, children see it as a real boat on a grand voyage. They imagine it crossing rivers, reaching distant lands, and having adventures. This is what makes childhood play so special - children can turn simple things into magical experiences. The poet captures this beautiful quality of childhood imagination.

faqs.question: बारिश के मौसम में और कौन-कौन से खेल खेलते हैं?
faqs.answer: Besides making paper boats (कागज़ की नाव), children enjoy many rainy season activities: बारिश में भीगना (getting wet in the rain), छतरी लेकर चलना (walking with an umbrella), कीचड़ में खेलना (playing in mud), पानी में छपाक-छपाक करना (splashing in water), and इंद्रधनुष देखना (watching rainbows). In India, the rainy season (बरसात) is a special time for children to enjoy nature!

faqs.question: 'तुकबंदी' क्या होती है? What is rhyming?
faqs.answer: तुकबंदी (Tukbandi) means rhyming - when two or more words have matching ending sounds. For example: बनाओ - तैराओ (both end in -ाओ), पानी - नानी (both end in -ानी). Rhyming makes poems musical and fun to recite. It also helps us remember the poem easily. When you read this poem aloud, listen for the matching sounds at the end of lines - that is तुकबंदी!

faqs.question: 'तैरना' और 'डूबना' में क्या अंतर है?
faqs.answer: तैरना (tairna) means 'to float' or 'to swim' - staying on top of or moving through water. डूबना (doobna) means 'to sink' or 'to drown' - going below the surface of water. These are opposite words (विलोम शब्द). The paper boat तैरती (floats) on water because it is light. If it gets too wet, it may डूब (sink). Other examples: लकड़ी तैरती है (wood floats), पत्थर डूबता है (stone sinks).

faqs.question: कविता में 'दोहराव' (repetition) क्यों किया गया है?
faqs.answer: The repetition (दोहराव) of 'नाव बनाओ, नाव बनाओ' serves several purposes: (1) It creates a strong, catchy rhythm (लय) that makes the poem musical. (2) It emphasises the excitement - saying it twice shows how eager the children are. (3) It makes the poem easy to remember and fun to recite. (4) It sounds like children calling out to each other to join the activity. Repetition is a common poetic device (काव्य अलंकार) used in many poems and songs.

faqs.question: क्या मैं अपनी खुद की कविता लिख सकता/सकती हूँ?
faqs.answer: Of course! Anyone can write a poem! Here are some tips: (1) Pick a topic you love - rain, games, food, family. (2) Write short lines about it. (3) Try to make the last words of some lines rhyme (तुकबंदी). (4) Read it aloud to hear the rhythm. Example: बारिश आई ज़ोर-ज़ोर / खुल गए सब खिड़की-दरवाज़े और / नाव बनाई कागज़ की / चल पड़ी वह दूर बहुत दूर! Start simple and have fun!

faqs.question: भारत में बारिश का मौसम कब आता है और इसे क्या कहते हैं?
faqs.answer: In India, the rainy season is called बरसात (barsaat) or वर्षा ऋतु (varsha ritu). It usually comes from June to September, when the monsoon (मानसून) winds bring rain from the sea. This season is very important for farming as crops need rainwater. In Hindi, we also call it सावन (Saavan) - the month associated with heavy rains. Many Hindi poems, songs, and festivals celebrate the rainy season because it brings greenery, relief from heat, and joy to children!
